message:
fix(indexer): force since_ts=None in watch-once mode so old messages are found regardless of mtime watermarks

When explicit watch_once_paths are provided, the reindex_paths function
was using the stored last_scan_ts watermark to filter scanned files by
mtime. This caused old conversations (whose session files have ancient
mtimes) to be silently skipped when the user explicitly requested them
via --watch-once.

Fix: treat watch-once the same as force_full for since_ts computation —
set since_ts = None so the connector scans the file unconditionally.
Also extract the repeated opts.watch_once_paths.as_ref().is_some_and()
check into a local `explicit_watch_once` variable for clarity and reuse.

New test:
- reindex_paths_watch_once_ignores_file_mtime_since_ts: creates a
  session file with an ancient createdAt timestamp, sets the Amp
  connector's last_scan_ts watermark to a high value, then verifies
  that watch-once still imports the conversation (indexed == 1)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
